content.full_text:
REED & DAVIDSON
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

DANA W. REED
CARY DAVIDSON

OF COUNSEL
DARRYL N. WOLD
BRADLEY W. HERTZ

COSTA MESA, CALIFORNIA 92626

TELEPHONE (714) 641-1668
FACSIMILE (714) 546-1003

LOS ANGELES OFFICE
777 SOUTH FIGUEROA STREET
SUITE 3400
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90017
TELEPHONE (213) 624-6200
FACSIMILE (213) 623-1692

October 16, 1992

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

Written advice is hereby requested pursuant to Government Code
Section 83114(b).

FACTS

I serve as a member of the Orange County Transportation Authority
Board of Directors. As such, I am a "designated employee"
pursuant to 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18730 and
must file an annual Statement of Economic Interest (Form 730).

It has been my policy since joining the Board to refuse gifts
aggregating $50 or more from any source covered by my disclosure
category.

Earlier this year, I accepted an invitation from a nonprofit, tax-
exempt business league, to attend a meeting of their board of
directors which was being held at a hotel in Orange County.

The event included tennis and golf in the afternoon. Prior to
dinner, cocktails were provided. Wine was served with dinner,
and an orchestra performed for those who wished to dance after
dinner.

I did not participate in either the golf or tennis activities.
Since I do not drink alcoholic beverages, I did not consume any
wine or participate in the cocktail reception other than as an
observer. I attended the dinner event by myself, ate a salad,
entree and dessert, had a cup of coffee, and listened to some of
the music but did not dance.

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
October 16, 1992
Page Two


Recently, to my great surprise, I received notification from the
sponsor that, according to their calculations, my pro rata share
of the cost of the event was a sum in excess of $50. Given my
policy of not accepting gifts aggregating $50 or more from a
single source, I would not have attended the event had I known
that my portion of the expenses would be greater than $50.

QUESTION

Does the Political Reform Act allow me to calculate and report
the fair market value of what I actually consumed at this event
or am I required to report the amount the sponsor determined was
my pro-rata share of the entire event?

Thank you for your consideration of this request.

Sincerely,

[signature]
David W. Reed

DWR:ljp

[illegible]

Thank you for your consideration of this request.
